round1: Round 1 is done, and it seems most of you still don't understand hockey. While Steph managed to correctly call the Capitals choking, and Nickall actually saw Detroit prevail, the rest of you collectively put your faith in Washington, New Jersey, and Phoenix, which worked out about as well as a goalie trying to save a puck with his face. Jaclyn, your Colorado pick was also a real head-scratcher.
round2: Well, Round 2 saw the mighty Penguins and Bruins fall, and most of you went down with them. Nickall and Derrick were the only ones who dared to pick the upsets, proving that sometimes even a blind squirrel finds a nut. The rest of you? Back to the drawing board, because this round was a clinic in how not to pick.
round3: Round 3 is over, and frankly, most of you should stick to watching figure skating. San Jose got swept, Montreal lost in 5, and only Derrick and Steph managed to pick a single correct winner. Better luck next time, or just admit you're terrible at this.
round4: Alright, the Blackhawks took home the Cup in 6 games. Marc, Jaclyn, and Nickall, congrats on not completely screwing up. Steph, your Flyers pick was about as useful as a goalie with no pads.
overall: The 2010 playoffs are in the books, with Chicago taking home the Cup, and our pool champions, Marc, Jaclyn, and Nickall, proving that sometimes mediocrity is all it takes to win, all tying with a measly 15 points. For Nickall, this shared victory, his first ever after 17 agonizing years, finally breaks his embarrassing championship drought, while Jaclyn ends a 12-year dry spell and Marc adds another title to his collection, if you can even call 15 points a 'title'. Meanwhile, Derrick, with a spectacular display of ineptitude, managed a paltry 4 points, securing his fifth utterly deserved last-place finish, proving he's consistently inconsistent. Stephanie also gets a nod for her impressive ability to pick zero correct teams, somehow still managing to avoid the absolute bottom by a single, pitiful point.
